mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This effect manifests over a 20-day horizon as market participants reprice growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 labor market recovery from COVID-19 trough
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window, or positive PAYEMS surprises fail to precede positive SPX returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins to accelerate, real rates turn negative, reducing the opportunity cost of holding equities relative to cash. This compression of the equity risk premium produces sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: negative real rates with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5%) when both trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.04 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening demand expectations and reduced future construction activity, which depresses equity valuations through lower expected corporate earnings in cyclical sectors. This negative signal emerges over a 5-day horizon as momentum traders react to the data release.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns following HOUST declines > 5% are positive in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC between HOUST momentum and 5-day returns exceeds -0.02 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates when supply-side capacity utilization rises, signaling pricing power and margin expansion for equities. Sustained INDPRO growth over 3 months predicts positive 20-day forward returns as investors reprice earnings quality upward.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 INDPRO at 106.3127, above pre-pandemic levels
regime_hint: supply-side recovery with capacity utilization normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month growth and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window, or positive INDPRO trends fail to precede positive SPX returns in > 52% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series accelerate simultaneously, it indicates broad-based demand expansion that supports equity valuations. This interaction effect produces positive 20-day returns as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 RSAFS at 619923.0 million, HOUST at 1739.0 thousand
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean < 1.0%) when both trigger conditions are met, or the interaction term's Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 6.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which depresses equity valuations through lower expected corporate earnings and consumer spending. This negative signal manifests over a 60-day horizon as the market reprices recession risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 UNRATE at 6.1%, elevated relative to pre-pandemic levels
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with rising unemployment
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in 3 months, or Rank IC between UNRATE changes and 60-day returns exceeds -0.02 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates while the federal funds rate remains unchanged, the real policy rate becomes increasingly negative, creating a monetary policy shock that compresses equity risk premiums. This mechanism produces negative 5-day returns as the market reprices inflation expectations and policy response risk.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change > 0.8% AND FEDFUNDS unchanged for 2+ months
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 inflation acceleration with FEDFUNDS at 0.07%
regime_hint: inflation surprise with accommodative monetary policy lag
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in > 60% of instances when both trigger conditions are met, or the interaction term's Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly provides a lagged but comprehensive signal of economic demand strength. Sustained positive GDP growth above 2% annualized signals healthy demand fundamentals that support equity valuations over a 60-day horizon through improved earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2021 GDPC1 at 19087.568 billion chained 2017 dollars, post-recovery trajectory
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive GDP growth momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 8-quarter rolling window, or positive GDP growth periods fail to precede positive SPX returns in > 55% of instances.
